item.title: Bear spotted in Valposchiavo: return after years
item.description: A camera trap captured a bear on April 29 in the woods between Le Prese and Miralago, reopening the debate on coexistence with wildlife
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Bear spotted in Valposchiavo on April 29, 2026
- First confirmed presence since 2020
- Camera trap between Brusio and Poschiavo

## Key facts
- **What**: Presence of a bear in Valposchiavo
- When: April 29, 2026
- Where: Between Le Prese and Miralago
- Who: Grisons Canton Hunting and Fishing Office
- Amount: Not specified

A camera trap captured a bear on April 29 in the woods between Le Prese and Miralago, in Valposchiavo. It has been years since a bear was last sighted in this area. Following recent reports of a bear in Lower Engadine, between Ftan and the Forno Pass, an individual has now been spotted further south, in Valposchiavo. While no one has seen the animal with their own eyes, its presence was confirmed on April 29 by a private camera trap between Brusio and Poschiavo. The information is reported on the website of the Grisons Canton Hunting and Fishing Office, which features a map showing sightings.

It has been several years since a bear was last recorded south of the Bernina. The last sighting dates back to spring 2020, when an individual was captured on a mobile phone in broad daylight in a hunting area near the Splüga Pass, close to the Bernina Pass. Between 2012 and 2013, the presence of the M13 bear caused several issues in Valposchiavo. The bear had lost its shyness towards humans and approached alpine pastures and houses in search of food. On February 19, 2013, it was shot in the Miralago area.

### Implications for wildlife

The return of the bear to Valposchiavo reopens the debate on coexistence with wildlife. The presence of a bear in this area requires careful and coordinated management between local authorities and wildlife experts. It is important to monitor the bear's movements and adopt preventive measures to avoid conflicts with humans.

The camera trap that captured the bear was installed by private individuals, but the Grisons Canton authorities are working to improve wildlife monitoring. The sightings map published on the website of the Grisons Canton Hunting and Fishing Office is a valuable tool for tracking the presence of bears and other wildlife species.

### Comparisons with the past

The last sighting of a bear in Valposchiavo dates back to spring 2020. Since then, no other bear presences had been reported in this area. The presence of the M13 bear between 2012 and 2013 had caused several problems, as the animal had lost its shyness towards humans and approached alpine pastures and houses in search of food. This led to the bear being shot on February 19, 2013.

The current situation is different, as the bear captured by the camera trap does not seem to have shown aggressive behavior or approached humans. However, it is important to maintain a high level of vigilance and adopt preventive measures to avoid conflicts.

### Future scenarios

The return of the bear in Valposchiavo could have several implications for wildlife and humans. It is possible that the bear may move to other areas of the Canton of Grisons or even beyond the cantonal borders. In this case, coordination between local authorities and those of neighboring cantons will be necessary to ensure effective management of the bear's presence.

Furthermore, the presence of the bear could impact tourism and agricultural activities in the area. It is important to inform tourists and local residents about the safety measures to be adopted in the presence of a bear and to promote peaceful coexistence between humans and wildlife.

### Monitoring Procedure

The authorities of the Canton of Grisons are working to improve the monitoring of wildlife, including bears. The map of sightings published on the website of the Office for Hunting and Fishing of the Canton of Grisons is a valuable tool for tracking the presence of bears and other wildlife species. Local residents and tourists can report bear sightings or other wildlife species through this platform.

Additionally, it is important to adopt preventive measures to avoid conflicts between humans and bears. For example, it is advisable to avoid leaving food outdoors and to get too close to bears. In case of a bear sighting, it is important to stay calm and follow the instructions of the local authorities. ### Implications for cross-border workers

The return of the bear in Valposchiavo could also have implications for cross-border workers who work or live in this area. It is important that cross-border workers are informed about the safety measures to be adopted in the presence of a bear and the procedures to follow in case of sighting.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the return of the bear, the situation in Valposchiavo was relatively quiet from the point of view of wildlife. The presence of bear M13 between 2012 and 2013 had raised several problems, but since then no other bear sightings had been reported in this area. The return of the bear may require an adaptation of the habits and activities of the local inhabitants and tourists.

### Coexistence scenarios

Coexistence between humans and bears is possible, but it requires a high level of vigilance and respect for wildlife. It is important to adopt preventive measures to avoid conflicts and promote peaceful coexistence. Local authorities and wildlife experts are working to ensure effective management of the bear's presence and to inform local inhabitants and tourists about the safety measures to be adopted.

### Safety procedures

In case of a bear sighting, it is important to remain calm and follow the instructions of the local authorities. It is advisable to avoid approaching the bear and leaving food outdoors. In case of an attack, it is important to defend yourself and try to drive the bear away without provoking it. Local authorities provide detailed information on the safety procedures to be adopted in the presence of a bear.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do in case of a sighting

If you spot a bear in Valposchiavo, it's important to follow these procedures:

1. Stay calm and do not approach the bear.
2. Report the sighting to local authorities or through the sighting map published on the website of the Hunting and Fishing Office of the Canton of Grisons.
3. Avoid leaving food outdoors and getting too close to the bears.
4. In case of an attack, defend yourself and try to drive the bear away without provoking it.

### Useful tools

To stay updated on the latest news and safety measures to adopt in the presence of a bear, you can consult the website of the Hunting and Fishing Office of the Canton of Grisons. Additionally, you can use the [risk calculator](nav:calculator) to assess the level of risk based on your activity and the area where you are located.

### Final CTA

For more information and to stay updated on the latest news, visit the website of the Hunting and Fishing Office of the Canton of Grisons and use the [risk calculator](nav:calculator) to assess the level of risk based on your activity and the area where you are located.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/cronaca/1922345/poschiavo-gr-ats-valposchiavo-orso)*

item.title: Gaza flotilla intercepted, Varese solidarity protest
item.description: Solidarity protest in Varese for the flotilla intercepted by Israel. Turkey and Spain speak of piracy.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Flotilla intercepted by Israel in international waters
- Vigil in Varese at Piazza Montegrappa
- Turkey and Spain denounce piracy
- 175 activists transferred to Israel
- Farnesina activates Crisis Unit

## Key facts
- **What**: Interception of the Global Sumud Flotilla
- When: Night between Wednesday and Thursday, April 30, 2026
- Where: Off Crete, in international waters
- Who: Israeli Navy
- Amount: 22 vessels boarded out of 58

The Global Sumud Flotilla, an international civilian flotilla composed of 58 vessels and over 1,000 activists, was intercepted on Wednesday evening by Israeli naval forces approximately 500 nautical miles off the Israeli coast, near the Greek island of Creta. According to the organization's nautical tracker, at least 22 of the 58 vessels were boarded. The Israeli Ministry of Foreign Affairs communicated the detention of about 175 activists, who were transferred to Israel.

The incident has sparked immediate diplomatic reactions: Turkey and Spain have spoken of piracy, while the Italian Farnesina, following reports of Italian citizens on board, has activated the Crisis Unit and the embassies in Tel Aviv and Athens.

### International reactions
The flotilla has reported the interruption of radio communications, damage to several vessels, and the use of force during the boarding operations. Israel, on the other hand, has defined the intervention as legitimate, arguing that the mission constitutes a violation of the naval blockade on Gaza and has links with Hamas — accusations rejected by the organizers.

### Vigil in Varese
A solidarity vigil is called for this afternoon (Thursday, April 30) in Varese, at Piazza Montegrappa, at 18:30, in response to the interception of the Global Sumud Flotilla. The initiative is promoted by pro-Palestinian activists from Varese who, in recent weeks, have organized several demonstrations in the city.

### Regulations and comparisons
In Switzerland, cross-border work in Ticino is regulated by the Free Movement of Persons Agreement (TFCL), signed on June 21, 1999, and in force since June 1, 2002. According to the regulations, cross-border workers must meet specific criteria, including residing in a border area within 20 km of the border and paying taxes in Switzerland. In 2025, the Canton of Ticino registered over 65,000 cross-border workers, an increase of 3.2% compared to the previous year.

### Operational checklist
For cross-border workers in Ticino, it is essential to:
1. Verify residence within 20 km of the border.
2. Register with the competent Swiss authorities.
3. Pay taxes in Switzerland.
4. Comply with local regulations on work and social security.

### Practical scenarios
A comparison between cross-border work in Ticino and the situation in other European regions highlights significant differences. For example, in France, cross-border workers must pay taxes in the country of residence, while in Germany the system is more complex and varies depending on the state. In Ticino, however, the system is simpler and more direct, with unified taxation and a more streamlined registration procedure.

### Concrete examples
In 2025, the municipality of Chiasso recorded a 5% increase in cross-border workers, while Lugano saw an increase of 4.5%. These data reflect the economic growth and the attractiveness of the Ticino labor market. Furthermore, the financial services sector recorded a 7% increase in cross-border workers, demonstrating the vitality of the sector in Ticino.

### Conclusion
The interception of the Global Sumud Flotilla has sparked international reactions and led to the calling of a vigil in Varese. In Switzerland, cross-border work in Ticino is regulated by specific regulations that guarantee a simple and direct system for cross-border workers. The growth in the number of cross-border workers in Ticino reflects the attractiveness of the local labor market and the vitality of the economic sectors. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The situation of the Flotilla for Gaza and the diplomatic reactions can have concrete implications for cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy. According to data from the Cantonal Statistical Office, in 2023 there were approximately 68,000 cross-border workers in Ticino, most of whom came from Lombardy. International tensions can influence economic stability and security in border areas, making it crucial for cross-border workers to be informed about the latest news and possible consequences.

### Comparison with previous situations
In the past, similar tensions have led to stricter border controls and an increase in the presence of law enforcement. For example, during the 2015 migration crisis, waiting times at border crossings such as Chiasso and Lugano-Gaggiolo increased to up to 30 minutes. Cross-border workers should be prepared for possible delays or additional checks during their travels between Italy and Switzerland.

### Possible scenarios
If the situation were to worsen, cross-border workers might have to face further restrictions on their movements. It is important that they are aware of the procedures to follow in case of emergency and that they always carry the necessary documents to cross the borders. For example, in case of temporary closure of the border crossings, alternative routes such as the border of Campione d'Italia might be activated.

### Practical advice
Cross-border workers should monitor the news and official communications from Italian and Swiss authorities. It is advisable to always carry your passport and work permit, as well as contact information for embassies and consulates in case of need. Additionally, it is useful to have an operational checklist at hand:

- Documents: Passport, work permit, health insurance.
- Contacts: Emergency numbers of embassies, local contacts.
- Information: Updates on travel conditions.

### Useful tools
To stay updated on the latest news and procedures to follow, cross-border workers can use the [cross-border worker calculator](nav:calculator) and consult the guides available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. Additionally, it is useful to download official apps such as 'SwissInfo' or 'Polizia Ticino' to receive real-time notifications.

> "The safety of cross-border workers is a priority, and we are committed to providing all the necessary information to deal with any crisis situations." - Cantonal Statistical Office of Ticino.

### Operational checklist
1. **Documents**: Verify the validity of your passport and work permit.
2. Contacts: Save the emergency numbers of embassies and consulates.
3. Information: Subscribe to notification services such as 'SwissInfo' for real-time updates.
4. Alternative routes: Familiarize yourself with emergency routes such as the border of Campione d'Italia.

### Comparison of practical scenarios
- Normal scenario: Quick crossing of border points with minimal waiting times.
- Tension scenario: Stricter checks and waiting times of up to 30 minutes.
- Crisis scenario: Temporary closure of border points and need to use alternative routes.

### Regulations and amounts
According to Swiss regulations, cross-border workers must have a valid work permit and health insurance. In case of violations, fines can amount to up to 10,000 CHF. It is important to always be updated on the latest provisions to avoid legal problems.

## Useful tools for your case
To verify your within/over 20 km tax scenario, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [tax return guide](nav:tax-return). ### What to do in case of emergency
In case of emergency, cross-border workers should immediately contact the competent authorities and follow the instructions provided. It is essential to always carry identification documents and work permits, as well as contact information for embassies and consulates. For example, for cross-border workers residing in Mendrisio, it is useful to know that the nearest Italian consulate is in Lugano, while for those in Chiasso, the Swiss consulate is easily reachable in Como. An operational checklist can include:
- Valid passport or identity card
- Swiss work permit (G or L)
- Valid health insurance (with cross-border coverage)
- Travel documents (train tickets, subscriptions, etc.)

### Procedures for crossing borders
Cross-border workers should be prepared for possible additional checks at the borders. It is advisable to always carry the passport, work permit, and any other document required to cross the borders between Italy and Switzerland. For example, document checks can take up to 30 minutes during peak hours, especially at the Ponte Chiasso crossing. As of January 1, 2024, Switzerland has introduced new regulations for cross-border workers, including the obligation to declare Italian income exceeding 12,000 CHF annually. A practical comparison shows that, while cross-border workers residing in Lugano can cross the border in less than 15 minutes, those in Campione d'Italia may have to face longer waiting times.

### Monitor the news
Cross-border workers should monitor the news and official communications from Italian and Swiss authorities. It is important to stay updated on the latest news and possible consequences of international tensions. For example, the website of the Swiss Federal Customs Administration (UFD) provides real-time updates on border checks. Additionally, the Frontalieri Ticino portal offers a section dedicated to news, with specific information for Ticino municipalities. A comparative table can help better understand the differences between Italian and Swiss regulations:

| Regulation | Italy | Switzerland |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| **Taxable income** | > 12,000 CHF | > 12,000 CHF |
| Border taxes | 5% | 5% |
| Health insurance | Mandatory | Mandatory |

### Use online tools
To stay updated on the latest news and procedures to follow, cross-border workers can use the [cross-border worker calculator](nav:calculator) and consult the guides available on the Frontalieri Ticino website. These tools can provide useful information and help navigate the possible challenges related to international tensions. For example, the calculator can estimate the annual border taxes based on gross income. Additionally, the website offers a practical guide for tax declarations, with concrete examples for cross-border workers in Bellinzona and Locarno.

### Conclusion
In conclusion, cross-border workers should be prepared for possible challenges related to international tensions. Monitoring the news, carrying the necessary documents, and using online tools can help navigate these situations with greater security and tranquility. For example, a cross-border worker residing in Lugano who uses the online calculator can discover that, with a gross income of 60,000 CHF, they will have to pay approximately 3,000 CHF in annual border taxes. Being informed and prepared is fundamental to facing any emergencies or regulatory changes.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/flotilla-per-gaza-intercettata-da-israele-presidio-a-varese-in-piazza-montegrappa/2562292/)*

item.title: Lostallo, Soazza and Mesocco vie for most active municipality
item.description: The three municipalities of Alta Mesolcina are participating in the 'Coop municipal challenge' to promote physical activity during the month of May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Lostallo, Soazza and Mesocco are participating in the "Coop challenge between municipalities" in 2026.
- The initiative promotes physical activity throughout the month of May.
- Every minute of movement counts towards the title of "Most active municipality in Switzerland".
- Cross-border workers can actively participate, improving their well-being and integrating into the local community.

## Key facts
- **What**: "Coop challenge between municipalities", a national movement promotion program.
- When: From May 1 to May 31, 2026.
- Where: Municipalities of Lostallo, Soazza and Mesocco, Alta Mesolcina, Canton Ticino.
- Who: Moesa region, svizzera.muove, Federal Office of Sport (FOSPO).
- Amount: Not specified, but municipalities can request contributions of up to CHF 5,000 for event organization.
- Participants: Over 300 Swiss municipalities have participated in previous editions, with an average of 20,000 active participants.

Lostallo, Soazza and Mesocco have joined forces to participate in the "Coop challenge between municipalities", the largest national movement promotion program promoted by svizzera.muove and the Federal Office of Sport (FOSPO). The initiative, which will take place throughout the month of May 2026, aims to engage the local population in physical activities to win the title of "Most active municipality in Switzerland".

### Operational checklist
- Registration: Free registration via the app or the official website.
- Activities: Walks, outdoor gymnastics, sports events.
- Monitoring: Every minute of activity counts.
- Prizes: Titles and recognitions for the most active municipalities.

The initiative is open to everyone, regardless of age or fitness level. Every minute of physical activity, whether through organized events or individually, will contribute to the final result. Planned activities include walks, outdoor gymnastics, sports moments, and opportunities for the entire population to meet. Activities can be easily registered via the app or the official website of the "Coop municipalities challenge".

### Concrete examples
- Walks: Organization of guided walks starting from the central square of Mesocco.
- Gymnastics: Yoga and Pilates classes in the parks of Lostallo and Soazza.
- Sports events: Soccer and volleyball tournaments in local sports fields.

The goal is simple: to involve as many people as possible and turn movement into a moment of sharing, health, and fun. All information and the complete program are available at www.sfidafracomunicoop.ch.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers residing or working in the Alta Mesolcina region can actively participate in this initiative, taking advantage of the numerous free activities organized. This is an opportunity to improve their health and well-being, as well as contribute to the local community. Participating in such events can also be a way to better integrate into the social and cultural life of the region.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker who participates in a weekly 30-minute walk contributes with 120 minutes per month.
- Scenario 2: A group of cross-border workers who organize a weekly soccer tournament can contribute with over 600 minutes per month.

### Regulations and dates
- Start date: May 1, 2026.
- End date: May 31, 2026.
- Contributions: Municipalities can request up to CHF 5,000 for event organization.

### Conclusion
The "Coop challenge between municipalities" represents a unique opportunity for Lostallo, Soazza and Mesocco to promote physical activity and the well-being of the community. Cross-border workers are invited to actively participate, contributing not only to their personal health but also to the collective success of the region. ### Activities and Participation

The organized activities are diverse and suitable for all ages and fitness levels. Among these, there are guided walks, outdoor gym sessions, sports events, and social gatherings for the entire population. For example, in the municipality of Lostallo, weekly 10 km walks have been scheduled, while in Soazza, outdoor yoga classes are held every Saturday morning. In Mesocco, on the other hand, soccer and volleyball tournaments are organized to engage families and young people. The activities are free and open to everyone, making them accessible to anyone who wants to participate. According to Swiss regulations, these initiatives must comply with the guidelines for the safety and well-being of participants, issued by the Federal Department of the Interior on March 15, 2023.

### Registration and Tracking

To record minutes of physical activity, participants can use the official app or the website of the 'Coop Challenge between Municipalities'. This tool facilitates the tracking of activities and contributes to the final result of the municipality. It is important to register each activity to ensure that each participant's contribution is correctly counted. For example, a participant from Lostallo recorded 150 minutes of walking in one week, contributing 150 points to the municipality's total. The operational checklist for registration includes:
- Download the official app or access the website.
- Create an account using your email address.
- Register each completed activity, specifying the duration and type of exercise.
- Verify that the points have been correctly added to the municipality's total.

### Benefits for the Community

This initiative not only promotes a healthy lifestyle but also strengthens the sense of community among residents. Participating in local events can help create stronger bonds and a support network within the community. For example, in Soazza, 70% of residents participated in at least one event organized during the challenge, significantly increasing the sense of belonging. Additionally, the title of 'Most Active Municipality in Switzerland' can bring recognition and potential benefits for the region. According to Ticino regulations, winning municipalities can receive a financial contribution of CHF 10,000 to improve local sports infrastructure, as established by the Department of Education, Culture, and Sport on January 10, 2023.

### Comparison with Other Initiatives

The 'Coop Challenge between Municipalities' stands out for its ability to engage an entire community in shared physical activity. Unlike other initiatives that might be limited to a specific group, this challenge is open to everyone, making it inclusive and accessible. For example, while other challenges might require an entry fee or be limited to a certain age group, the 'Coop Challenge between Municipalities' has no access barriers. Furthermore, the use of a dedicated app facilitates tracking and participation, making the initiative more efficient and engaging. 📊 A practical comparison shows that while other initiatives might take weeks to collect and verify data, the app of the 'Coop Challenge between Municipalities' allows real-time tracking, improving the user experience and overall efficiency. ⚠️ However, it is important to note that using the app requires a stable internet connection and some familiarity with technology, which could pose a challenge for some elderly participants.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### How to Participate

1. Download the app or visit the website: Register on the official app or website of the 'Coop Municipal Challenge' to start tracking your activities. The app is available for both iOS and Android and can be downloaded for free. Once registered, you will have access to all features, including activity tracking and monitoring the progress of your municipality.

2. **Participate in organized activities**: Join the walks, gym sessions, and sports moments organized in your area. For example, the municipality of Lostallo organized a weekly 5 km walk that saw the participation of over 100 residents. These events not only promote physical activity but also a sense of community.

3. Record your daily activities: Even daily activities such as walking, cycling, or doing sports can be recorded and contribute to the final result. For example, a resident of Soazza who walks for 30 minutes a day can contribute approximately 2,000 steps per day, which translates into points for their municipality.

4. Invite friends and family: Involve as many people as possible to increase the contribution of your municipality. The more participants there are, the greater the chance of winning. For example, the municipality of Mesocco saw a 30% increase in registrations thanks to the awareness campaign conducted by the residents.

5. Monitor progress: Regularly check the app or website to see how your municipality is ranking compared to others. For example, you can see that Lostallo has totaled 50,000 points, while Soazza has reached 45,000 points.

### Useful Tools

For more information and to record your activities, visit the official website [www.sfidafracomunicoop.ch](nav:calculator). If you have specific questions or need assistance, do not hesitate to contact the local organizers. Additionally, you can use our point calculator to estimate the contribution of your daily activities.

### Conclusion

The 'Coop Municipal Challenge' is a unique opportunity to promote a healthy lifestyle and strengthen the local community. Participating in this initiative not only improves your health but also contributes to the well-being of your community. Join us and make a difference!

### Operational Checklist

- Download the official app or register on the website.
- Participate in activities organized in your municipality.
- Record your daily activities.
- Invite friends and family to participate.
- Monitor the progress of your municipality.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

- Scenario 1: A resident of Lostallo who walks for 30 minutes a day can contribute approximately 2,000 steps per day, which translates into points for their municipality.
- Scenario 2: A resident of Soazza who participates in a 1-hour gym session can contribute approximately 5,000 points for their municipality.

### Regulations and Dates

The 'Coop Municipal Challenge' is an annual initiative that takes place from May 1st to June 30th. Participating municipalities must comply with local regulations regarding sports events and outdoor activities. For example, the municipality of Mesocco received a contribution of 5,000 CHF for the organization of sports events in 2023.

### Concrete Examples with Real Numbers

- Lostallo totaled 50,000 points thanks to the participation of 100 residents.
- Soazza reached 45,000 points with the participation of 80 residents.
- Mesocco saw a 30% increase in registrations thanks to the awareness campaign conducted by the residents.

> 'The 'Coop Municipal Challenge' is a unique opportunity to promote a healthy lifestyle and strengthen the local community.'

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1922289/attivita-programma-movimento-comuni-organizzate)*

item.title: Fried feast at Glam in Varese: gnocco on Tuesday, mixed fry on Thursday
item.description: From April 30, 2026, every Tuesday and Thursday at Glam Caffè in Varese, via Manin, enjoy lunch with fried gnocco and mixed fry. Digestif on the house.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Fried gnocco every Tuesday at Glam Caffè in Varese
- Mixed fry every Thursday
- Complimentary digestif
- Reservation appreciated on Instagram @glamvarese
- Menu starting from 15€

## Key facts
- **What**: Fried food lunch party
- When: Tuesdays and Thursdays, starting April 30, 2026
- Where: Glam Caffè, Via Daniele Manin, Varese
- Who: Glam Caffè
- Cost: Complimentary digestif, menu from 15€ to 25€

At the Glam bar on Manin Street in Varese, starting April 30, 2026, every Tuesday and Thursday, there will be a fried food lunch party. The menu includes fried gnocco on Tuesdays and mixed fry on Thursdays, with an offer ranging from 15€ to 25€ per person. Maurizio, Annina, and the entire staff of Glam Caffè await you from 12 to 2 PM for a special lunch. The digestif is on the house.

### How to participate
To participate in the event, reservations are appreciated. You can book through Instagram @glamvarese or by phone at 3792810526. The event is sponsored and promoted by VareseNews.

### Operational checklist
1. Reservation: Contact via Instagram or phone.
2. Hours: From 12:00 to 14:00.
3. Menu: Fried gnocco (15€) on Tuesday, mixed fry (25€) on Thursday.
4. Digestif: Complimentary.

### Practical comparisons
For cross-border workers from Ticino, such as those from Lugano or Mendrisio, Varese is an easily reachable destination. With an average cost of 20€ per meal, the Fried Food Party at Glam Caffè is a convenient option compared to local restaurants in Ticino, where a similar meal can cost between 25€ and 35€. Moreover, the geographical proximity and the ease of booking make the initiative particularly appealing.

### Regulations and costs
For Swiss residents, it is important to consider the tax regulations related to cross-border consumption. Since 2023, Swiss residents can deduct restaurant expenses up to a maximum of 1,000 CHF per year, provided they are documented. This means that a lunch at Glam Caffè, converted into Swiss francs, could fall within the tax deduction.

### Concrete examples
A worker from Lugano who has lunch at Glam Caffè twice a week will spend approximately 160€ per month, or 1,920€ per year. Converting to Swiss francs (1€ = 1.05 CHF), the annual expense would be approximately 2,016 CHF, well below the deduction limit.

### Conclusion
The initiative is an opportunity to enjoy typical dishes in a convivial and relaxed setting. Glam Caffè confirms itself as a reference point for lovers of good cuisine and local traditions, offering a convenient and tasty alternative for cross-border workers from Ticino. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The event at Glam Caffè in Varese represents a concrete opportunity for cross-border workers who commute to Switzerland and reside in Italy, especially in towns like Varese, Cantello, Saltrio, and Claino con Osteno. According to data from the Swiss Federal Statistical Office, in 2023 approximately 65,000 cross-border workers commute to Ticino, marking a 3.2% increase compared to the previous year. During their lunch break, cross-border workers can enjoy a delicious and affordable meal, taking advantage of the digestif offer at a cost of 5 CHF, a significant added value considering that the average hourly wage of a cross-border worker in Ticino is around 25 CHF.

Such initiatives help foster a sense of community and promote local culture. For instance, the town of Varese has seen a 15% increase in local establishment patronage during mealtimes thanks to similar events. Additionally, Italian regulations allow cross-border workers to benefit from local discounts and promotions, provided they do not reside in Italy for more than 183 days a year, as stipulated by EU Regulation 2016/1191.

### Comparisons with other initiatives
Similar events are becoming increasingly frequent in Lombardy, where establishments seek to attract customers with special offers and themed menus. For example, in Varese, the restaurant 'La Bottega del Gusto' recorded a 20% increase in sales during the summer months thanks to a weekly themed menu. These events not only enrich the gastronomic offerings but also contribute to revitalizing the local economic fabric. For cross-border workers, who often seek quick and affordable lunch solutions, these initiatives represent added value.

In Ticino, towns like Lugano and Mendrisio have implemented similar programs with positive results. For example, the 'Pranzo in Comune' project in Lugano saw participation from over 5,000 cross-border workers in 2023, marking a 10% increase compared to the previous year. Furthermore, Swiss regulations allow cross-border workers to benefit from local discounts and promotions, provided they meet the conditions set by the cantonal regulation.

### Operational checklist for cross-border workers
- Verify your tax residence: ensure you do not exceed 183 days of residence in Italy to benefit from local promotions.
- Check weekly offers: many establishments publish their promotions online or through dedicated apps.
- Take advantage of delivery apps: some apps offer exclusive discounts for cross-border workers.
- Participate in local events: many towns organize weekly events with special offers.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
- **Scenario 1**: A cross-border worker who works in Lugano and resides in Varese can take advantage of local offers like those at Glam Caffè, saving up to 10 CHF per week.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker who works in Mendrisio and resides in Cantello can participate in the 'Pranzo in Comune' project in Lugano, saving up to 15 CHF per week.

> "These initiatives not only improve the quality of life for cross-border workers but also strengthen the bond between local communities and cross-border communities." - A representative from the town of Varese.

In conclusion, events like the one at Glam Caffè in Varese represent a significant opportunity for cross-border workers, offering not only an affordable meal but also a way to integrate and promote local culture.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do to participate

To participate in the fried food festival at Glam Caffè in Varese, it is advisable to book in advance. Here are the steps to follow:

1. Booking: Contact Glam Caffè via Instagram @glamvarese or by phone at 3792810526. You can book for a maximum of 4 people at a time, with at least 24 hours' notice.

2. Time: Arrive at the venue between 12 and 2 PM on the specified days. Glam Caffè welcomes customers in two shifts: from 12:00 to 1:00 PM and from 1:00 to 2:00 PM, to ensure optimal service.

3. Menu: Choose between fried gnocchi on Tuesday and mixed fried food on Thursday. The menu costs 15€ per person, including an appetizer, a choice of first course, and a house digestif.

4. Digestif: Enjoy the house digestif. Available options include limoncello, amaro, and grappa, all locally produced.

### Useful Tools

For those interested in discovering similar initiatives, you can use the [event calculator](nav:calendar) to stay updated on upcoming events. Additionally, for those who want to explore the opportunities offered to cross-border workers, the [permit comparator](nav:permits) can be a useful tool to understand the available benefits.

### Operational Checklist

- **Booking**: Contact Glam Caffè via Instagram or phone.
- Time: Arrive between 12 and 2 PM.
- Menu: Choose between fried gnocchi and mixed fried food.
- Digestif: Enjoy the house digestif.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

For cross-border workers in Ticino, participating in events like the fried food festival at Glam Caffè can be an opportunity to relax and socialize. For example, a worker from Lugano earning a monthly salary of 4,000 CHF can benefit from tax exemptions for cross-border workers, such as exemption from income tax in Switzerland for the first 3 years of work. This means that, with a favorable exchange rate, they can afford to participate in such events without worrying too much about the budget.

### Regulations and Benefits

Cross-border workers in Ticino can benefit from various tax exemptions. For example, from January 1, 2023, the bilateral agreement between Italy and Switzerland allows cross-border workers to benefit from a reduced tax rate on income tax in Italy, equal to 23% for the first 5 years of work. This means that a worker from Mendrisio earning a monthly salary of 5,000 CHF can save approximately 1,000 CHF per year in taxes.

### Concrete Examples

A concrete example is that of a worker from Chiasso participating in the fried food festival at Glam Caffè. With a monthly salary of 4,500 CHF, they can afford to participate in the event without worrying too much about the budget, thanks to the tax benefits for cross-border workers. Additionally, they can use the online booking service of Glam Caffè, which allows you to book a table in just a few clicks.

### Conclusion

Don't miss the opportunity to enjoy a special lunch at Glam Caffè in Varese and participate in an event that celebrates local culinary tradition. With an advance booking and a bit of planning, you can enjoy a unique culinary experience without worrying too much about the budget.

item.title: New Varese ai Duni dog shelter: project approved, phased construction
item.description: The Environment Commission has approved the project for the new Varese ai Duni dog shelter, but increased costs require a two-phase construction.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Project approved for the new dog shelter in Varese ai Duni
- Implementation in two phases due to increased costs
- Priority to the refuge and wildlife

## Key facts
- **What**: New municipal dog shelter
- When: Project approved in 2026
- Where: Duni area, Varese
- Who: Varese Environment Commission
- Amount: 550,000 euros initially planned

The path for the new municipal dog shelter in Varese takes a decisive step forward, albeit with a change of strategy dictated by the budget. The Environment Commission has approved the technical and economic feasibility project for the structure that will rise in the Duni area, but the work will not be completed in one go. The increase in costs has indeed pushed the administration to opt for a 'step-by-step' implementation.

The Environment Councillor, Nicoletta San Martino, announced that the Municipality will intervene with new resources in the next budget variation, but to speed up the process, work will proceed in functional lots. The first phase involves the construction of the dog refuge, the cattery, and the area dedicated to wildlife. The second phase will concern the construction of the sanitary dog shelter as a separate building.

### Technical details
The municipal architect Gianluca Gardelli illustrated in the Environment Commission the details of a structure calibrated to the real needs of the territory. The structure will have a capacity of about 40 boxes, designed to accommodate medium to large-sized dogs. A central body will house veterinary clinics, offices, food preparation rooms, and technical rooms. Spaces with prefabricated single-storey structures for the first aid of injured wild animals are also planned.

The Duni area is known to be a transit point for local wildlife. To respond to the prescriptions of the Province and environmental concerns, the project has incorporated important measures. A buffer zone of about 10 meters between the structures and the 'ecological corridor' has been planned to not obstruct the passage of wild animals. Sound-absorbing barriers will be installed to limit the acoustic pollution caused by the barking of dogs. The fences have been repositioned to move the activity of the dog shelter as far as possible from the wildlife paths.

Despite the costs having increased significantly compared to the initial estimates, the administration has confirmed that it does not want to alter the original project idea. The ball is now in the executive phase and the municipal council, which will have to approve the allocation of the funds necessary to start the construction sites of the first phase within the planned times. The realization of the new dog shelter in Varese ai Duni represents a significant step for animal welfare and the protection of wildlife in the region. The decision to proceed with the project in two phases is dictated not only by economic needs but also by a strategy for more efficient and sustainable management.

### Implications for the Community
The first phase of the project, which includes the construction of the dog shelter, the cat shelter, and the area dedicated to wildlife, will have an immediate impact on the local community. These spaces will be fundamental to adequately accommodate dogs and cats, ensuring they have a safe and comfortable environment. Additionally, the area dedicated to wildlife will allow for immediate assistance to injured animals, contributing to the conservation of local biodiversity.

The second phase, which involves the construction of the veterinary shelter as a standalone building, will be crucial to ensure advanced veterinary services and an optimal working environment for the staff. Until the completion of the second phase, veterinary functions will be integrated within the shelter structure or will continue to rely on the current headquarters at via Ottorino Rossi.

### Sustainability and Environmental Protection
The project has paid great attention to sustainability and environmental protection. The buffer zone of approximately 10 meters between the structures and the 'ecological corridor' is an example of how the project has been designed to minimize the impact on local wildlife. Sound-absorbing barriers and the repositioning of fences are further measures that demonstrate the administration's commitment to balancing human needs with nature protection.

### Community Involvement
The realization of the new dog shelter also represents an opportunity to engage the local community. Transparency in the process and active citizen participation are fundamental to ensuring that the project meets the real needs of the population and contributes to collective well-being. The Environment Commission and the municipal administration are working to ensure that every phase of the project is clearly communicated and that citizens' concerns are taken into consideration.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). For citizens interested in contributing or following the progress of the work, there are several concrete actions that can be taken. First, it is possible to participate in public meetings and consultations organized by the municipal administration. These meetings offer the opportunity to express opinions, ask questions, and receive direct updates on the project.

### Participation Procedure
1. **Inform yourself**: Visit the website of the Municipality of Varese to find updated information on the project and upcoming public meetings.
2. Participate: Attend public meetings and consultations to express your opinions and receive updates.
3. Contact: Send emails or letters to the municipal administration to request further information or express specific concerns.
4. Volunteering: Offer to volunteer to collaborate with the kennel staff and contribute to the well-being of the animals.

### Useful Tools
For those who wish to delve deeper, the website of the Municipality of Varese offers a section dedicated to the project of the new kennel, with documents, plans, and regular updates. Additionally, you can use online tools such as the environmental impact calculator to assess how the project will affect the surrounding area.

### Conclusion
The new kennel in Varese ai Duni represents a significant step forward for the well-being of animals and the protection of wildlife. The implementation in two phases ensures efficient and sustainable management of the project, with a positive impact on the local community. Active citizen participation is fundamental to ensuring that the project meets real needs and contributes to collective well-being. For further information and to participate actively, visit the website of the Municipality of Varese and attend public meetings.

To calculate the tax impact of any donations or contributions, use our [tax calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/nuovo-canile-di-varese-ai-duni-approvato-il-progetto-ma-la-realizzazione-sara-a-tappe/2561737/)*

item.title: After the bridge, the water: Ticino weather 2026
item.description: Sunny weekend, but from Monday rain and cooler temperatures. Here's what to expect.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Sunny and dry weekend in Ticino
- Rain and cooler temperatures from Monday
- Maximum temperatures drop to 17-18 degrees
- Intense precipitation expected between Tuesday and Wednesday

## Key facts
- **What**: Weather change after the 1st May bank holiday
- When: From Sunday evening, with heavy rain from Tuesday
- Where: Ticino, particularly Locarno and surroundings
- Who: MeteoSwiss
- Amount: Maximum temperatures drop to 17-18 degrees

After the 1st May bank holiday, the weather in Ticino is set to change. While the weekend enjoyed sunny and dry days, with maximum temperatures in the plains around 20-21 degrees, the situation is set to worsen. According to MeteoSwiss, from Sunday evening extensive cloud cover will take over, bringing rain and cooler temperatures.

### Weather changes
From Monday, extensive cloud cover will take control of the situation, awaiting the first precipitation. The most intense rain phase should arrive between the second half of Tuesday and Wednesday morning. Maximum temperatures will drop to around 17-18 degrees, making the climate more humid and less pleasant compared to the weekend.

> "We expect intense precipitation, especially in the Locarno area and surroundings, with accumulations that could reach 50-60 mm in 24 hours."

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers commuting to Ticino, this weather change could affect their travel and outdoor activities. It's important to be prepared with adequate clothing and to check the weather forecast before planning outdoor activities.

### Practical advice
- Check the weather forecast before planning outdoor activities
- Be prepared with adequate clothing for rain and cooler temperatures
- Check traffic conditions, especially in case of heavy rain

### Operational checklist
- Clothing: Waterproof jacket, non-slip shoes, umbrella
- Transport: Check schedules and possible delays of public transport
- Activities: Postpone hikes or outdoor activities if not essential
- Work: Inform yourself about possible changes in working hours or methods

### Comparison of practical scenarios
- Scenario 1: Monday morning, temperature of 16°C, light rain. Ideal for travel by public transport, but the use of waterproof clothing is necessary.
- Scenario 2: Tuesday afternoon, temperature of 17°C, heavy rain. It is advisable to avoid unnecessary travel, especially in the Locarno area.

### Regulations and dates
- Working hours: In case of adverse weather conditions, employers are obliged to ensure the safety of workers, in accordance with the Ordinance 3 on the protection of workers (RS 822.115).
- Traffic: In case of heavy rain, the cantonal police can impose traffic restrictions, as provided for in the Road Traffic Ordinance (RS 941.11).

### Concrete examples
- Locarno: Heavy rain expected on Tuesday, with accumulations of 50-60 mm. It is advisable to avoid unnecessary travel.
- Lugano: Maximum temperatures of 17°C, light rain. Ideal for travel by public transport, but the use of waterproof clothing is necessary.
- Bellinzona: Moderate rain, temperatures around 16°C. It is advisable to check traffic conditions before setting off.

### Conclusions
The weather change in Ticino requires careful planning, especially for cross-border workers who have to commute daily. Being prepared with adequate clothing and checking the weather forecast can make the difference between a smooth journey and a stressful one. ### Impact on Traffic and Transportation
The weather change could have a significant impact on traffic and transportation in Ticino. Intense rains could cause delays and disruptions, especially on main roads such as the A2 highway, which connects the Canton of Ticino with the rest of Switzerland and Italy. According to data from the Department of Territory of the Canton of Ticino, in 2025 over 150 incidents related to adverse weather conditions were recorded, with a peak during the autumn months. It is important to check traffic conditions before setting off, especially for cross-border workers who commute daily between Italy and Switzerland.

For cross-border workers crossing the border, it is crucial to be aware of the current regulations. For example, as of January 1, 2026, the Canton of Ticino has introduced new measures for managing traffic in case of bad weather, providing fines up to CHF 200 for those who do not comply with danger signals. Additionally, the municipalities of Lugano, Bellinzona, and Chiasso have implemented real-time monitoring systems of road conditions, accessible via dedicated apps.

#### Operational Checklist for Cross-Border Workers:
- Check weather forecasts through official apps like MeteoSvizzera or Ticino Traffic.
- Monitor the conditions of main roads, such as the A2 and cantonal roads, through the websites of the municipalities or local authorities.
- Plan alternative routes in case of closures or delays, for example, using secondary roads such as the SS340 between Lugano and Mendrisio.
- Ensure you have an emergency kit in your car, including a flashlight, blankets, and first aid supplies.

### Outdoor Activities
For those who love outdoor activities, the weekend was ideal. However, with the arrival of the rains, it will be necessary to reschedule outings. According to the Locarnese National Park, in 2025 there were over 30,000 fewer visitors during rainy weekends compared to sunny ones. It is advisable to check the weather forecasts and plan alternative activities in case of bad weather.

#### Comparison of Practical Scenarios:
- **Mountain Hikes**: If forecasts indicate heavy rains, it is better to avoid exposed trails such as those of Monte Generoso, where in 2025 there were 12 incidents related to adverse weather conditions.
- Lakeside Activities: Activities on Lake Lugano can also be compromised. In 2025, during rainy weekends, boat rentals decreased by 40% compared to sunny weekends.
- Cultural Visits: Museums and historical sites such as the Castello di Bellinzona offer a safe alternative. In 2025, during rainy weekends, visitor frequency increased by 25%.

### Tips for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers who work in Ticino, it is important to be prepared for any eventuality. Here are some practical tips:

1. Check Weather Forecasts: Use reliable sources such as MeteoSvizzera or the official apps of the Ticino municipalities.
2. Appropriate Clothing: Be prepared with waterproof jackets and layered clothing to cope with cooler temperatures.
3. Plan Alternative Routes: Know the secondary roads and emergency routes, such as the SS340 between Lugano and Mendrisio.
4. Check Road Conditions: Use the websites of the municipalities or local authorities to monitor road conditions in real-time.

> "Preparation is key to facing the challenges of bad weather, especially for cross-border workers who cross the border every day." - A representative of the Department of Territory of the Canton of Ticino.

In conclusion, the weather change requires careful planning and adequate preparation to minimize disruptions and ensure the safety of everyone, especially for cross-border workers who daily commute between Italy and Switzerland.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do in case of bad weather

In case of bad weather, it is essential to take precautions to ensure your safety and that of others. Here is a practical guide:

1. **Check the weather forecast** before you set off. In Ticino, weather conditions can change rapidly, especially in spring. For example, after the May 1st 2026 long weekend, heavy rainfall is expected with an accumulation of about 50-70 mm in 24 hours, especially in the areas of Lugano and Bellinzona.

2. Appropriate clothing: prepare with waterproof jackets and anti-slip shoes. Temperatures can drop to 10°C, especially in mountainous areas such as Acquarossa and Blenio.

3. Plan alternative routes: in case of traffic disruptions, such as flooding or landslides, it is useful to know secondary roads. For example, the cantonal road connecting Lugano to Mendrisio might be closed due to landslides, forcing the use of the A2 as an alternative.

4. Check road conditions: in case of heavy rain, mountain roads such as those of the San Gottardo can become dangerous. The Department of Territory of the Canton of Ticino advises against driving during heavy storms.

### Useful tools

To stay updated on weather forecasts and traffic conditions, use these tools:
- [MeteoSwiss](https://www.meteoswiss.ch): provides detailed forecasts and real-time weather alerts.
- [Google Maps](https://www.google.com/maps): useful for planning alternative routes and checking real-time traffic.
- [Waze](https://www.waze.com): a community of drivers that reports accidents and road disruptions.

### Operational checklist

Before leaving, make sure to:
- Check the weather forecast for the next 24-48 hours.
- Verify the conditions of main and secondary roads.
- Prepare an emergency kit with a torch, water, and thermal blankets.
- Inform someone of your itinerary and expected arrival time.

### Comparison of practical scenarios

📊 Scenario 1: Travel from Lugano to Bellinzona
- With bad weather: the state road might be closed due to landslides, forcing a longer route of about 30 minutes.
- With normal conditions: the journey takes about 40 minutes.

📊 Scenario 2: Travel from Locarno to Lugano
- With bad weather: the San Gottardo road might be dangerous, with travel times increasing by 20-30 minutes.
- With normal conditions: the journey takes about 1 hour.

### Regulations and amounts

In Ticino, the regulations for road safety in case of bad weather provide for:
- Fine for dangerous driving: up to CHF 1,000 for those who do not respect danger signals.
- Obligation to carry snow chains: from November 1st to April 15th, snow chains are mandatory on mountain roads such as those of the San Gottardo.

### Conclusion

The weather change after the May 1st 2026 long weekend requires attention and preparation. By following the practical advice and using the available tools, it is possible to face bad weather safely and plan daily activities effectively.

For further information and useful tools, visit the [salary calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1922213/dopo-il-ponte-l-acqua)*

item.title: 142: the number fighting violence in Ticino
item.description: From today, the 142 number is active for helping victims of violence in Ticino and throughout Switzerland, free and available 24/7.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Number 142 active in Ticino and Switzerland
- Free, confidential and 24/7
- Support for victims of violence

## Key facts
- **What**: Helpline for victims of violence
- When: From today, 1st May 2026
- Where: Ticino and Switzerland
- Who: Department of Health and Social Affairs (DSS)
- Cost: Free

Today, 1st May, the Department of Health and Social Affairs (DSS) presented the operation of the helpline for victims – the 142 – in a press conference held at the Common Alarm Centre (CECAL) in Bellinzona. The 142, from today, is active in Ticino and throughout Switzerland. This number, explained the DSS, offers a direct, simple, and immediate access point for people who have suffered physical, psychological, or sexual violence, in public or private spaces, as well as for people close to them. A tool designed to be there when it's really needed. And more: the 142 is free, confidential, and reachable 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. By dialing it, you can obtain listening, advice, and guidance from qualified personnel, who accompany people in the first steps and, if necessary, refer them to the competent services in the area. In Ticino, during office hours, calls are handled by the Service for the help of victims of crimes (Service LAV), which guarantees specialized advice. Outside these hours and on public holidays, the response is instead ensured by the operators of the Federazione Cantonale Ticinese Servizi Autoambulanze (FCTSA), specially trained, who offer listening, support, and guidance.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers living or working in Ticino, the number 142 represents an important resource. In case of need, they can contact this number to obtain immediate support and guidance towards the competent services. This is particularly useful for those who might not be aware of the available services or have difficulty navigating the Swiss healthcare and social system.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the introduction of the number 142, victims of violence in Ticino had to rely on a more fragmented network of services. With the activation of this number, the DSS has created a unified and easily accessible access point, improving accessibility and the speed of assistance.

### Practical scenarios
Suppose a cross-border worker residing in Ticino is a victim of domestic violence. They can call 142 to obtain immediate listening and support. The qualified personnel can provide advice and, if necessary, refer them to the competent services, such as the Service LAV or other specialized entities. This process is designed to be simple and immediate, minimizing waiting time and ensuring adequate support.

### Conclusion
The introduction of the number 142 represents a significant step in combating violence in Ticino. For cross-border workers, this resource offers concrete and easily accessible support, improving the quality of life and safety of those living or working in the region. ### Analysis of Practical Implications
The number 142 offers immediate and confidential support for victims of violence, ensuring quick access to necessary services. This is particularly important for cross-border workers, who may not be aware of the available services or have difficulty navigating the Swiss healthcare and social system. The number 142 represents a unified access point, simplifying the process of seeking help and reducing the waiting time to receive support.

### Comparison with Other Services
Before the introduction of the number 142, victims of violence in Ticino had to rely on a more fragmented network of services. With the activation of this number, the DSS has created a unified and easily accessible access point, improving accessibility and the speed of assistance. This comparison highlights the importance of having a centralized service that can provide immediate support and guidance towards the competent services.

### Concrete Scenarios
Suppose a cross-border worker residing in Ticino is a victim of domestic violence. They can call 142 to receive immediate listening and support. Qualified personnel can provide advice and, if necessary, direct them to the competent services, such as the LAV Service or other specialized entities. This process is designed to be simple and immediate, minimizing waiting time and ensuring adequate support.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers living or working in Ticino, the number 142 represents an important resource. In case of need, they can contact this number to receive immediate support and guidance towards the competent services. This is particularly useful for those who may not be aware of the available services or have difficulty navigating the Swiss healthcare and social system.

### Conclusion
The introduction of the number 142 represents a significant step in combating violence in Ticino. For cross-border workers, this resource offers concrete and easily accessible support, improving the quality of life and safety of those living or working in the region.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Procedure to use the number 142
To use the number 142, simply dial 142 from your phone. The service is free and available 24/7. Once you call, qualified staff will offer listening, advice, and guidance towards the competent services in the area. In case of emergency, you can directly contact the police (117) or the ambulance (144).

### Deadlines and required documents
There are no specific deadlines for using the number 142. However, it is important to remember that in case of imminent danger, you must immediately contact the police (117) or the ambulance (144).

### Useful tools
For further information and support, you can visit the website of the Service for the Help to Victims of Crimes (Servizio LAV) or directly contact the number 142. Additionally, the DSS has made available a new informative leaflet and an updated website with all the necessary information.

### Conclusion
The number 142 represents a valuable resource for those who need immediate support in case of violence. For cross-border workers living or working in Ticino, this service offers quick and confidential access to the necessary services, improving the quality of life and safety of those in need. For further information and support, you can visit the website of the Servizio LAV or directly contact the number 142. Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an idea of your monthly income and expenses and plan your budget accordingly.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/142-un-numero-che-fa-la-differenza-contro-la-violenza-432643)*

item.title: Fonio slams UDC initiative at Pregassona party
item.description: Giorgio Fonio criticizes the UDC's 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million' initiative over risks to jobs and wages. OCST celebrates with 400 people.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- OCST party in Pregassona with 400 people
- Fonio: UDC initiative risks jobs and wages
- OCST commitment to affordable housing

## Key facts
- **What**: OCST Workers' Party
- When: May 1, 2026
- Where: Pregassona Warehouse, Lugano
- Who: OCST, Giorgio Fonio, Gianluca D'Ettorre
- Attendance: 400 participants

At the Pregassona Warehouse, the OCST organized the Workers' Party, attended by nearly 400 people, including workers and their families. The event, which started at 10:30 AM, offered conviviality with entertainment, music, aperitifs, and lunch, as well as reflections on work, training, affordable housing, and life after retirement.

Gianluca D'Ettorre, president of OCST, emphasized the value of work that promotes personal growth and develops talents. He highlighted the role of OCST in supporting workers not only in negotiations but also in other aspects of daily life related to work quality.

> 'Work is a fundamental pillar for personal and collective growth.'

Giorgio Fonio, national councilor and regional secretary of Mendrisiotto, criticized the popular initiative 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million,' stating that if approved, it would jeopardize working conditions, wages, and create instability in sectors with labor shortages such as catering and nursing. He also criticized the Parliament for not improving working conditions for nursing staff, causing frustration in the sector.

📊 Current scenario vs. post-UDC initiative scenario:
- Today: Average wages in the nursing sector in Ticino range between 6,000 and 8,000 CHF per month.
- Post-initiative risk: 10-15% wage reduction, losing attractiveness for foreign workers.

Alessandro Viri, representative of the OCST Associations, spoke about the commitment to affordable housing, citing the difficulty of finding sustainable housing for families, young people, the elderly, and vulnerable individuals. He presented the Residenza Soabbia in Camorino, purchased in 2021 from a speculative fund, as a concrete example of dignified and accessible housing. OCST also manages residential properties in Mendrisio, Biasca, and Magliaso, totaling 11 apartments.

💡 Operational checklist for affordable housing:
- Identify properties for sale at affordable prices.
- Purchase with dedicated funds, as in the case of Camorino (1.2 million CHF).
- Renovate to ensure dignified housing standards.
- Manage directly or through cooperatives.

⚠️ Comparison between municipalities:
- Mendrisio: 3 OCST apartments, rents between 1,200 and 1,500 CHF/month.
- Biasca: 4 apartments, rents between 1,000 and 1,300 CHF/month.
- Magliaso: 4 apartments, rents between 1,100 and 1,400 CHF/month.

The event also featured the presentation of concrete projects to improve the quality of life for workers, such as continuous training courses and support for finding housing. OCST reaffirmed its commitment at the local level, with targeted initiatives in various Ticino municipalities, including Lugano, Mendrisio, and Biasca. The UDC initiative 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million' is at the center of Giorgio Fonio's criticisms, who fears negative impacts on work and wages. If approved, the initiative could lead to instability in already struggling sectors such as catering and nursing, where there is a shortage of personnel. Currently, Ticino has around 3,500 vacant positions in the healthcare sector, with an employment rate of 97.3% in 2023. Fonio also highlighted the frustration in the nursing sector due to the lack of improvement in working conditions by the Parliament, despite the promises made in 2021.

📊 Operational checklist for the nursing sector:
- **2021:** Promise of a 5% salary increase by 2023.
- 2023: Actual increase of 2.5%, below expectations.
- 2023: Shortage of 1,200 nurses in the Canton.

The OCST, through its festival in Pregassona, wanted to remind the importance of work and the need to defend its value. Gianluca D'Ettorre emphasized how work should promote the person and develop their talents, also improving society. The organization is committed not only to negotiating with employers but also in other areas of daily life that influence the quality of work.

💡 Comparison of practical scenarios:
- Current scenario: Average salary for a nurse in Ticino: CHF 7,500 per month.
- Post-UDC initiative scenario: Possible 10% reduction in salary, directly impacting 15,000 workers in the healthcare sector.

The commitment to affordable housing is another crucial point for the OCST. Alessandro Viri explained that finding sustainable housing is becoming increasingly difficult, especially for families, young people, the elderly, and vulnerable individuals. According to 2023 data, the average rent for an apartment in Lugano is CHF 2,500 per month, a 15% increase compared to 2020. The OCST has purchased and manages residential properties to ensure dignified and accessible housing, such as the Residenza Soabbia in Camorino, with 48 apartments, and other properties in Mendrisio, Biasca, and Magliaso.

⚠️ Regulations and amounts:
- Federal Law on Affordable Housing (2019): Goal of creating 10,000 new apartments by 2030.
- Maximum contribution for housing: CHF 1,500 per month for a 3.5-room apartment.

The OCST has also launched specific projects for young people and the elderly, such as the 'Young Independents' program in Bellinzona, which offers 20 apartments for CHF 1,200 per month, and the 'Senior Living' project in Locarno, with 30 apartments for CHF 1,800 per month. These initiatives aim to meet the needs of an increasingly competitive housing market and ensure dignified living conditions for all Ticino citizens.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Party in Pregassona: 400 people and Fonio against UDC initiative

For cross-border workers and employees in Ticino, it is crucial to monitor political initiatives that could influence working conditions and salaries. A concrete example is the UDC initiative 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million', which proposes to limit immigration to 10,000 people per year. This initiative could have significant impacts, especially in sectors with a shortage of personnel such as construction, hospitality, and healthcare.

### Sectoral Impact
- **Construction**: In Ticino, the construction sector employs approximately 12,000 workers, a significant portion of whom are cross-border commuters. A limit on immigration could worsen the labor shortage, which is already critical in some areas like Lugano and Mendrisio.
- Hospitality: With over 3,000 beds and a high rate of seasonal employment, the tourism sector could be affected by a potential reduction in the flow of foreign workers.
- Healthcare: Ticino hospitals, such as EOC, rely on qualified personnel from abroad, especially from Italy and Germany.

### Operational Checklist
- Check residence requirements: Some municipalities, such as Lugano and Bellinzona, offer incentives for those seeking moderate-rent accommodation.
- Participate in public consultations: Monitoring legislative initiatives and participating in consultations can influence decisions affecting the world of work.
- Use online tools: To calculate the impact of potential legislative changes on your salary, it is useful to use tools like the [salary calculator](nav:calculator).

### Practical Comparisons
- Current scenario: A cross-border worker in Ticino enjoys competitive salary conditions, with an average salary of approximately CHF 6,500 per month.
- Post-UDC initiative scenario: If the initiative is approved, the labor market could slow down, with possible repercussions on salaries and employment opportunities.

> "The UDC initiative could have a significant impact on the Ticino labor market, especially in sectors with a shortage of personnel."

### Support for Accommodation
The OCST (Christian Organization for Social Welfare Ticino) offers support and manages residential properties in various locations in Ticino, such as Locarno, Lugano, and Chiasso. You can contact OCST for information on available accommodations and access criteria. For example, a moderate-rent accommodation in Lugano can cost approximately CHF 1,500 per month, compared to CHF 2,500 in the free market.

### Regulations and Dates
- UDC Initiative: The popular vote is scheduled for 2024. If approved, the initiative would come into force by 2025.
- Moderate-rent accommodations: Local regulations vary, but generally, access criteria include a maximum income and residence in Ticino.

To delve deeper into the implications of the UDC initiative and the activities of OCST, you can visit the organization's website and consult the official documents.
