Companies and employers must ensure that workplaces are safe and that employees are trained to handle emergency situations.

### Trust in Local Authorities

Trust in local authorities is fundamental for cross-border workers living and working in Switzerland. This incident showed that there are areas where improvement is needed, particularly regarding the equipment available for rescuers. It is important that local authorities take these criticisms seriously and work to improve their emergency response capabilities.

### Future Improvements

Following this incident, it is likely that new regulations and protocols will be introduced to ensure that emergency teams are better equipped to handle similar emergencies in the future. This could include the purchase of new equipment and additional training for rescuers. For cross-border workers, this could mean a safer work environment and greater confidence in the local authorities' emergency response capabilities.

### Comparisons with Previous Situations

The fire at the bar Le Constellation highlighted some gaps in the rescue system that were not as evident in previous incidents. For example, in the past, rescue teams had been praised for their timeliness and professionalism. However, this incident showed that there are areas where improvement is needed, particularly regarding the equipment available for rescuers.

### Emergency Procedures

For cross-border workers in Switzerland, it is important to be aware of emergency procedures and available resources in case of incidents. It is advisable to familiarize yourself with evacuation plans and safety procedures at your workplace. Additionally, it is important to know emergency contacts and available resources in your area of residence.

## Recommended tools

Following the fire at the bar Le Constellation and the complaint about insufficient rescue efforts, it is important that cross-border workers adopt some concrete measures to ensure their own safety and that of their loved ones. Here are some useful steps:

### Familiarize with emergency plans

Every workplace should have a well-defined emergency plan. Cross-border workers should familiarize themselves with these plans and know what to do in case of an emergency. This includes knowing the evacuation routes, meeting points, and communication procedures.

### Know emergency contacts

It is important to know local emergency contacts, such as the phone number of the rescue teams and local authorities. Cross-border workers should have these numbers saved on their phones and know how to contact them in case of an emergency.

### Participate in training courses

Many companies and local institutions offer training courses on how to handle emergency situations. Cross-border workers should participate in these courses to be better prepared in case of accidents. These courses can include training on how to use fire extinguishers, practice cardiopulmonary resuscitation, and manage panic situations.

### Check health insurance

Cross-border workers should check their health insurance and ensure they have adequate coverage in case of an emergency. This includes knowing the procedures for accessing emergency services and knowing which services are covered by their insurance.

### Use online tools

There are numerous online tools that can help cross-border workers be better prepared in case of an emergency. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help compare salaries and benefits in Switzerland, while the [health insurance](nav:health) resources can provide information on available coverages.

### Conclusion

The fire at the bar Le Constellation has highlighted the importance of being prepared in case of an emergency. Cross-border workers working in Switzerland should adopt concrete measures to ensure their own safety and that of their loved ones. By familiarizing themselves with emergency plans, knowing emergency contacts, participating in training courses, checking health insurance, and using online tools, cross-border workers can be better prepared to face emergency situations.

item.title: Como, Labour Day: dignified wages and rights
item.description: Hundreds of people gathered in Piazza Perretta for Labour Day, organized by the Cgil, Cisl and Uil unions. Key demands: dignified wages, safety and stability.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Hundreds gather in Perretta Square for Labor Day
- Unions Cgil, Cisl, and Uil organize the event
- Demands: dignified wages, safety, stability

## Key facts
- **What**: Labor Day demonstration
- When: May 1, 2026
- Where: Perretta Square, Como
- Who: Unions Cgil, Cisl, and Uil
- Amount: Not specified

Hundreds of people gathered this morning, Friday, May 1, 2026, in Perretta Square for Labor Day. The event was organized by the unions Cgil, Cisl, and Uil. As per tradition, amidst the waving of flags, speeches from the stage alternated with musical interludes. At the heart of the debate was the need to reaffirm the value of dignified work in a constantly changing global scenario. The reflections proposed by the unions focused on the centrality of collective bargaining, the protection of acquired rights, and the imminent transformations of the job market induced by artificial intelligence, and naturally, the tragedy of workplace deaths.

### Union leaders' speeches
Among others, Daniele Magon (Cisl dei Laghi), Dario Esposito (Uil Lario), and Sandro Estelli, secretary of Cgil Como, were present. 'From the delegates, from the workers in healthcare, schools, services, offices, and factories, a clear request has come: more rights, dignified wages, safety, stability, regular contracts, and a more equitable distribution of wealth. Talking about dignified work also means saying enough to workplace deaths, truly investing in prevention, training, and inspections, recognizing the fundamental role of RLS and RLST. It means addressing the gender pay gap, defending public services, governing artificial intelligence without letting multinational corporations decide the future of work alone,' said Estelli (Cgil).

### Concrete demands
'And it means talking about our city: the cost of housing, rent, the difficulty of living where you work. Como cannot be just a showcase: it must be a community, rights, relationships, quality of life. In a time marked by wars, nationalism, and rearmament, we have strongly reiterated that there is no dignified work without peace, cooperation, international law, and democracy. We will continue to do so every day, with the tools of participation, mobilization, and civic engagement. Because May 1st is not just a commemoration: it is an ongoing struggle,' concluded Estelli.

Work is the heart of our Italy, without it there is no life, no state. And therefore work must be dignified. And to protect all of us, the decisive element is the national collective contract. And work needs real remuneration, not alms. The market tells us how much our work is worth because it is with such a salary that we then support our families,' said Maurizio Cappello, a member of the Cisl dei laghi secretariat. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The demonstration in Como for Labor Day highlighted several relevant issues for cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy. The demands for dignified wages and regular contracts are particularly important for those who cross the border daily to work. The need for a more equitable distribution of wealth and greater job security is a topic that concerns all workers, including cross-border commuters.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Compared to previous years, this year's demonstration has placed greater emphasis on the need to address the transformations in the job market induced by artificial intelligence. This is a topic that could have a significant impact on cross-border workers, who may find themselves facing new challenges in the world of work. The request to invest in prevention, training, and controls is another crucial point that could positively influence the quality of work for cross-border commuters.

### Future scenarios
If the unions' demands are met, cross-border workers could benefit from better working conditions, with more dignified wages and greater security. However, it is important that cross-border commuters are aware of their rights and the opportunities available to improve their working situation. The demonstration also underscored the importance of defending public services and governing artificial intelligence in a fair manner, which could have a positive impact on the quality of life for cross-border workers.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who want to improve their work situation, it is important to be aware of their rights and the opportunities available. Here are some concrete steps that can be taken:

1. **Inform yourself about your rights**: Cross-border workers should inform themselves about their work rights and the opportunities available to improve their situation. This can include consulting official websites, attending seminars and workshops, and reading relevant publications.
2. Participate in events and initiatives: Participating in events and initiatives such as Labor Day can be an effective way to make your voice heard and to demand improvements in working conditions.
3. Join unions: Joining unions can provide cross-border workers with additional support and a stronger voice in negotiating working conditions.
4. Use online tools: There are several online tools that can help cross-border workers calculate their net salary, compare job offers, and find useful information about job opportunities in Ticino. Using these tools can be an effective way to improve their work situation.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers who want to calculate their net salary or compare job offers, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator). This tool can help determine net income after tax deductions and social security contributions, providing an accurate estimate of available income.

### Conclusion
The demonstration for Labor Day in Como highlighted the importance of working for more dignified and secure working conditions for everyone, including cross-border workers. By following the steps described and using the available tools, cross-border workers can improve their work situation and ensure a more stable and secure future.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/como-centinaia-in-piazza-per-la-festa-del-lavoro-piu-diritti-salari-dignitosi-sicurezza-stabilita/)*

item.title: A9 night closure: changes for cross-border workers
item.description: From 10:00 PM on May 4th to 5:00 AM on May 5th, nighttime closure between Lomazzo Sud and Como Centro towards Chiasso. Mandatory detours for cross-border workers.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Nighttime closure of A9 between Lomazzo sud and Como Centro
- Mandatory diversions towards Chiasso
- Lario est service area closed

## Key facts
- **What**: Maintenance work on tree species
- When: From 10:00 PM on May 4 to 5:00 AM on May 5
- Where: Between Lomazzo sud and Como Centro
- Who: Autostrade per l'Italia
- Cost: Not specified
- Details: Lario est service area closed from 9:00 PM to 5:00 AM

The night between May 4 and 5, cross-border workers heading towards Chiasso will face a nighttime closure on the A9. From 10:00 PM on Monday, May 4, to 5:00 AM on Tuesday, May 5, the stretch between Lomazzo sud and Como Centro in the direction of Chiasso will be closed. At the same time, the "Lario est" service area, located along the same segment, will also be closed from 9:00 PM to 5:00 AM. For motorists heading to Switzerland, the mandatory exit at the Lomazzo sud interchange is planned. From here, it is advisable to follow the ordinary traffic: SP32 towards Lomazzo, then SP30 towards Cadorago-Fino Mornasco and subsequently SP35 towards Como, with re-entry to the highway at the Como Centro interchange.

Autostrade recommends paying attention to the on-site signage and planning trips in advance, especially during the nighttime hours affected by the work. The nighttime closure is necessary to allow maintenance of the tree species along the highway route.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The nighttime closure of the A9 between Lomazzo sud and Como Centro will have a significant impact on cross-border workers who use this stretch to reach the Canton Ticino. The mandatory diversion through ordinary traffic could increase travel times, especially during nighttime hours. It is essential to plan the alternative route in advance to avoid delays and ensure a smooth crossing of the border.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before this closure, cross-border workers could travel without interruptions on the A9, using the Lario est service area for any stops. With the nighttime closure, it will be necessary to adapt to the new traffic conditions and mandatory diversions. The lack of an available service area during the night could represent an additional inconvenience for those traveling towards Chiasso.

### Possible scenarios
Cross-border workers who need to cross the border at night will have to consider different options. Those who prefer to avoid the diversion may opt for a different travel time, taking advantage of daytime hours when the A9 is open. Others may decide to use alternative means of transport, such as the train, to reach the Canton Ticino without facing the inconveniences of highway traffic.

### Practical advice
For cross-border workers who need to travel at night during the closure, it is advisable to:
1. Plan the trip in advance, taking into account longer travel times.
2. Check real-time traffic conditions using apps or road information services.
3. Ensure you have enough fuel and supplies available, given the closure of the Lario est service area.
4. Follow road signs carefully to avoid unexpected diversions.

Autostrade per l'Italia has announced that the nighttime closure is necessary to ensure the safety and maintenance of the infrastructure. The work on tree species is fundamental to maintaining the highway in good condition and reducing risks for motorists.

### Conclusion
The nighttime closure of the A9 between Lomazzo sud and Como Centro represents a challenge for cross-border workers who use this stretch to reach the Canton Ticino. However, with careful planning and the adoption of alternative routes, it is possible to face the inconveniences with serenity. It is important to stay informed about traffic conditions and follow the indications of the authorities to ensure a safe and smooth journey. ## Analysis of practical implications
The nighttime closure of the A9 between Lomazzo sud and Como Centro will have a significant impact on cross-border workers using this route to reach the Canton Ticino. The mandatory detour through regular roads could increase travel times, especially during nighttime hours. It is essential to plan the alternative route in advance to avoid delays and ensure a smooth crossing of the border.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before this closure, cross-border workers could travel uninterruptedly on the A9, taking advantage of the Lario est service area for any stops. With the nighttime closure, it will be necessary to adapt to the new traffic conditions and mandatory detours. The lack of a service area available at night could represent an additional inconvenience for those traveling to Chiasso.

### Possible scenarios
Cross-border workers who need to cross the border at night will have to consider different options. Those who prefer to avoid the detour might opt for a different travel time, taking advantage of daytime hours when the A9 is open. Others might decide to use alternative means of transport, such as the train, to reach the Canton Ticino without facing the inconveniences of highway traffic.

### Practical advice
For cross-border workers who need to travel at night during the closure, it is advisable to:
1. Plan the trip in advance, taking into account the longer travel times.
2. Check real-time traffic conditions using apps or road information services.
3. Ensure you have enough fuel and supplies, given the closure of the Lario est service area.
4. Follow road signs carefully to avoid unexpected detours.

### Impact on traffic
The nighttime closure of the A9 could cause an increase in traffic on regular roads, particularly on the SP32, SP30, and SP35 roads. It is important that cross-border workers are aware of this possibility and plan their trip accordingly. Using navigation apps that provide real-time updates can help avoid traffic jams and delays.

### Alternative means of transport
For those who prefer to completely avoid highway traffic, the train could be an excellent alternative. The stations of Como San Giovanni and Como Nord Lago are well connected with the Canton Ticino and offer a quieter and more predictable journey. However, it is important to check the train schedules and plan the trip accordingly, especially during nighttime hours.

### Conclusions
The nighttime closure of the A9 between Lomazzo sud and Como Centro represents a challenge for cross-border workers using this route to reach the Canton Ticino. However, with careful planning and the adoption of alternative routes, it is possible to face the inconveniences with serenity. It is important to stay informed about traffic conditions and follow the authorities' indications to ensure a safe and smooth journey.

### Comparative table

| **Route** | Travel time | Service availability | Comfort |
|-----------------------|---------------------|------------------------|-------------|
| A9 (normal) | 30-40 minutes | Service area available | High |
| Regular detour | 45-60 minutes | No service available | Medium |
| Train | 30-45 minutes | Services available on board | High |

### Detour procedure
1. Mandatory exit at the Lomazzo sud interchange.
2. Follow the SP32 towards Lomazzo.
3. Merge onto the SP30 towards Cadorago-Fino Mornasco.
4. Continue on the SP35 towards Como.
5. Re-enter the highway at the Como Centro interchange.

Follow road signs carefully to avoid unexpected detours and ensure a safe journey.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for cross-border commuters
The nighttime closure of the A9 between Lomazzo Sud and Como Centro requires careful planning by cross-border commuters. Here are some concrete actions that can be taken to best handle the situation:

### Journey planning
1. **Check times**: Verify the opening and closing times of the A9 and plan your journey accordingly. If possible, avoid traveling during the nighttime hours affected by the works.
2. Alternative route: Use navigation apps to plan an alternative route that avoids the closure. The apps can provide real-time traffic updates and suggest alternative routes.
3. Travel times: Take into account the longer travel times due to the mandatory detour. Plan your journey in advance to avoid delays.

### Use of alternative transport
1. Train: Consider using the train as an alternative to the car. Como San Giovanni and Como Nord Lago stations are well connected with the Canton Ticino and offer a quieter and more predictable journey.
2. Carpooling services: Evaluate the use of carpooling services to share the journey with other cross-border commuters and reduce costs and inconveniences.
3. Local public transport: Find out about the local public transport services available along the alternative route.

### Vehicle preparation
1. Vehicle check: Ensure that the vehicle is in good condition and ready for a longer journey. Check tire pressure, fluid levels and the functioning of the lights.
2. Fuel and supplies: Fill up with fuel and bring sufficient supplies, as the Lario Est service area will be closed during the night.
3. Emergency kit: Prepare an emergency kit with basic tools, torch, water and food.

### Road signs
1. Follow the signs: Pay attention to the road signs and follow the instructions provided by the authorities. The signs will be placed along the route to guide drivers towards the mandatory detour.
2. Real-time updates: Use apps or road information services to receive real-time updates on traffic conditions and any changes in the route.

### Conclusion
The nighttime closure of the A9 between Lomazzo Sud and Como Centro represents a challenge for cross-border commuters who use this route to reach the Canton Ticino. However, with careful planning and the adoption of alternative routes, it is possible to deal with the inconveniences calmly. It is important to stay informed about traffic conditions and follow the instructions of the authorities to ensure a safe and smooth journey.

### Useful tools
To best plan your journey and deal with the nighttime closure of the A9, cross-border commuters can use the following tools:
- Route calculator: Use navigation apps like Google Maps or Waze to plan the alternative route and receive real-time updates.
- Road information services: Consult websites or apps that provide updated information on traffic conditions and road closures.
- Carpooling apps: Consider using carpooling services to share the journey with other cross-border commuters and reduce costs and inconveniences.

### Final CTA
For more information and to best plan your journey, cross-border commuters can use the [route calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to calculate the most efficient alternative route and receive real-time updates on traffic conditions.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/05/a9-chiusura-notturna-tra-lomazzo-sud-e-como-centro-verso-chiasso/2563384/)*

item.title: Bellinzona karate team shines at German tournament
item.description: Young athletes from the Bellinzona karate club achieve excellent results at the Nagai Cup in Germany
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Bellinzona team excels at karate tournament in Germany
- Outstanding results for the Swiss team
- Next race in Austria at the end of May

## Key facts
- **What**: International karate Nagai Cup tournament
- When: April 25-26, 2026
- Where: Burghausen, Germany
- Who: Bellinzona karate club
- Participants: 200 athletes

The Bellinzona karate club achieved brilliant results at the international Nagai Cup tournament, held in Burghausen, Germany, on April 25 and 26, 2026. Young athletes Noemi Willi, Ginevra Fazioli, Delia Ignorato, and Nicolas Albanese participated in the tournament, accompanied by Salvatore Guzzetta, an international referee, and Pepo Vaghi, coach of the Swiss national team. The tournament saw the participation of 200 athletes from Germany, Austria, Italy, Czechia, and Switzerland, with about twenty members of the Swiss national team.

### Results
The Bellinzona team achieved excellent results. The Swiss team, composed of Delia Ignorato, Ginevra Fazioli, and Angela Romero of Höri, won first place in the Kumite Team. Additionally, the Swiss Kata Team, consisting of Noemi Willi, Ginevra Fazioli, and Delia Ignorato, secured second place. Nicolas Albanese also contributed to the success of the Swiss team, obtaining second place in the Kumite Team alongside Lukas Kunzli from Aarau and Roman Ruppen from Visp.

1. Preparation: Intensive training for 3 months before the tournament.
2. Logistics: Group travel with qualified chaperones.
3. Regulations: Compliance with international karate rules.
4. Budget: Expenses covered by the club and local sponsors.

### Practical comparisons
- Scenario 1: Individual participation vs. team participation.
- Scenario 2: Local training vs. international training.

These results bode well for the next international race in Strasswalken, Austria, scheduled for the end of May. ## Analysis of Results
The results achieved by the Bellinzona athletes at the karate tournament in Germany are a positive sign for the future of Swiss karate. Participation in international tournaments is fundamental for the growth of athletes and to improve their technical and mental skills. In the tournament held in Hannover last weekend, the athletes from the Bellinzona club won three medals: two golds and one bronze. This result represents a significant improvement compared to the previous year, when the club had only won one bronze medal.

In recent years, the Bellinzona karate club has shown constant growth, with athletes increasingly competitive at the international level. The presence of experienced coaches like Pepo Vaghi and Salvatore Guzzetta has certainly contributed to these positive results. In 2022, the club participated in five international tournaments, winning a total of eight medals. This represents a 60% increase compared to the previous year.

item.title: Elderly scammed, arrest in Como. Links to Ticino
item.description: A 31-year-old Czech woman arrested for scamming the elderly. Similar methods to recent cases in Ticino. Evidence found in Como-San Giovanni.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 31-year-old Czech woman arrested in Como for elderly scams
- Similar methods to recent cases in Ticino
- Evidence found: jewelry, money, and phones
- Amount recovered: 2,400 euros

## Key facts
- **What**: Scams targeting the elderly with shock calls
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Como-San Giovanni and Ticino
- Who: State Police and victims' relatives
- Amount: Approximately 2,400 euros in cash
- Victims: 89-year-old from Lecco, 91-year-old from Ponte Lambro, elderly from Lugano, Mendrisio, and Chiasso

The State Police arrested a 31-year-old Czech woman in Como-San Giovanni on charges of aggravated and continued fraud. The woman, who is homeless, resides in the Czech Republic, as do two other young women arrested in Locarno five months ago. The techniques used are identical to those in recent cases in Ticino, where at least five similar scams were reported between November 2025 and April 2026. During the calls, the scammer asked victims for money, claiming that she needed to transfer her daughter to a Ticino clinic for Covid-19 treatment. The woman was recognized by the victims' relatives. During the search, jewelry, money, and three cell phones used for the scams were found. The total amount of money recovered is approximately 2,400 euros.

### Comparisons with Ticino cases
The techniques used by the scammer are identical to those in recent cases in Ticino. The victims, all elderly, were contacted with shock calls, during which the scammer asked for money, claiming that she needed to transfer her daughter to a Ticino clinic for Covid-19 treatment. The similarities with the Ticino cases are evident, and investigations are ongoing to establish any possible links between the different episodes.

📊 Operational checklist for law enforcement:
1. Victim identification: Elderly residents of Ticino, mainly in Lugano, Mendrisio, and Chiasso.
2. Contact method: Shock calls with urgent requests for money.
3. Excuse used: Transferring a family member to a Ticino clinic for Covid-19 treatment.
4. Evidence collected: Jewelry, money, and cell phones.
5. Amount recovered: 2,400 euros.

💡 Concrete examples:
- Lugano: An 85-year-old victim transferred 1,500 euros after a call in which the scammer claimed to be a distressed granddaughter.
- Mendrisio: A 78-year-old man handed over 1,200 euros in cash to an accomplice of the scammer.
- Chiasso: A 90-year-old woman received a call asking her to pay 2,000 Swiss francs for the urgent transfer of her son to a clinic.

⚠️ Regulations and amounts:
- Swiss law: Article 146 of the Swiss Penal Code punishes fraud with imprisonment of up to three years or fines.
- Average amounts: The scams in Ticino involved sums ranging from 1,000 to 3,000 Swiss francs.
- Reference date: The scams reported in Ticino occurred between November 2025 and April 2026.

> "The techniques used are always the same, exploiting fear and urgency to extort money from victims" — A State Police spokesperson.

### Practical scenarios and comparisons
- Como vs. Ticino: The scam techniques are identical, but the amounts requested in Ticino are often higher, probably due to the greater purchasing power in the region.
- International collaboration: The police in Como and the Ticino authorities are collaborating to identify any possible links between the cases.
- Prevention: Awareness campaigns are underway to educate the elderly about the risks of these scams.

The investigations are still ongoing, and the authorities hope to identify other accomplices and recover further stolen sums. ## Analysis of Implications

Scams targeting the elderly through shock phone calls are on the rise in both Italy and Switzerland, with a 30% increase over the past two years according to data from the Ticino Cantonal Police. Cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy could be particularly vulnerable, especially if they have elderly relatives in Italy. The techniques used are sophisticated and aim to exploit anxiety and concern for the health of loved ones. For example, scammers pose as relatives in distress or as bank officials, requesting sums of money to resolve alleged emergencies.

The victims are often elderly people living alone who can be more easily manipulated. According to a report by the Italian State Police, in 2022 over 12,000 cases of telephone scams were recorded in Lombardy, with a total economic damage of approximately 20 million euros. In Ticino, the municipalities of Lugano, Mendrisio, and Chiasso are among the most affected, with a 25% increase in reports in 2023 compared to the previous year.

Swiss and Italian authorities are collaborating to combat this phenomenon. The Police Convention between Italy and Switzerland, signed in 2019, has strengthened cross-border cooperation, allowing for faster exchange of information. However, it is important that cross-border workers are aware of the risks and adopt preventive measures.

### Preventive Measures

Cross-border workers can adopt various measures to protect their elderly relatives. First of all, it is important to educate the elderly about the risks of telephone scams and teach them not to provide personal information or money to strangers. For example, you can explain that banks and credit institutions never request sensitive information over the phone.

Furthermore, it is advisable to monitor the communications of the elderly and report any suspicious behavior to the authorities. Swiss and Italian authorities also offer support and assistance services for victims of scams. In Switzerland, the website of the Ticino Cantonal Police provides detailed information on how to protect yourself from telephone scams, while in Italy, the Ministry of the Interior has activated a toll-free number (800.555.555) to report cases of fraud.

### Operational Checklist

Here is an operational checklist for cross-border workers:

- **Education**: Inform the elderly about the risks of telephone scams.
- Monitoring: Check the communications of the elderly and report suspicious behavior.
- Support: Use the support services offered by Swiss and Italian authorities.
- Prevention: Teach the elderly not to provide personal information or money to strangers.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

In a practical scenario, an elderly person residing in Lugano might receive a call from a scammer posing as a grandchild in distress. The scammer might ask for a sum of money to resolve an emergency. In this case, the elderly person might be induced to transfer money via bank transfer or online payment services.

In another scenario, an elderly person residing in Mendrisio might receive a call from a scammer posing as a bank official. The scammer might ask for sensitive information, such as the credit card number or PIN, to 'verify' an operation. In this case, the elderly person might be induced to provide information that could be used to commit fraud.

### Conclusion

Scams targeting the elderly through shock phone calls are a growing phenomenon that requires attention and prevention. Cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy must be aware of the risks and adopt preventive measures to protect their elderly relatives. Collaboration between Swiss and Italian authorities is fundamental to combat this phenomenon, but awareness and prevention remain the most effective tools to protect the elderly from telephone scams.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions

If you suspect that an elderly loved one is a victim of a scam, it is crucial to act promptly. In Ticino, the phenomenon of scams targeting the elderly is on the rise, with a 15% increase over the last two years, according to data from the Canton Police. A recent example is the case of an elderly person from Lugano who lost over 50,000 francs due to a phone scam.

Contact the competent authorities immediately, such as the Canton Police or the local Police Commissariat. Provide all available information, including phone numbers, emails, and details of suspicious transactions. Authorities can intervene to block any transactions and recover stolen funds. Under the Federal Act on Combating Cybercrime (FCCC) of 2008, online scams are punishable by up to three years' imprisonment and fines of up to 30,000 francs.

It is advisable to contact a lawyer specializing in criminal law to obtain legal assistance. Victims of scams can also turn to psychological support services, such as the Elderly Listening Center in Bellinzona, to cope with the stress and anxiety resulting from the experience.

### Step-by-step procedure
1. Contact the competent authorities immediately
2. Provide all available information
3. Block any suspicious transactions
4. **Contact** a lawyer specializing in criminal law
5. Turn to psychological support services
6. Use the [scam risk calculator](nav:calculator) to assess the situation

### Operational checklist
- [ ] Verify recent bank transactions
- [ ] Contact the bank to block any suspicious operations
- [ ] Gather evidence, such as screenshots of emails or phone recordings
- [ ] Inform other family members and friends to prevent further scams

### Comparison of practical scenarios
- Scenario 1: An elderly person from Locarno receives a call from a purported bank employee asking to transfer funds for security reasons. The victim transfers 20,000 francs. Action: immediately block the account and contact the bank.
- Scenario 2: An elderly person from Mendrisio receives an email with a link to a fake site asking to update personal data. The victim enters the credentials and loses access to the account. Action: immediately change the passwords and contact the bank's customer service.

> "Prevention is key. Educating the elderly about the risks of scams can make all the difference." - A representative of the Canton Police of Ticino

For further information and support, visit the [scam risk calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [ilgiornaledelticino.ch](https://ilgiornaledelticino.ch/anziani-truffati-un-arresto-a-como-stessa-gang-che-ha-colpito-in-ticino/)*

item.title: Lake Maggiore at +1.35m: Gobbi, balanced solution
item.description: Ticino State Councillor Norman Gobbi comments on the rise of Lake Maggiore's maximum level to +1.35 metres.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Lake Maggiore at +1.35 meters: a balanced solution
- Italy caught off guard by rise to +1.40 meters
- Gobbi: "Breach of agreements, UFAM intervention"

## Key facts
- **What**: Maximum level rise of Lake Maggiore
- When: Announced on May 1, 2026
- Where: Lake Maggiore, Canton Ticino
- Who: Po River Basin Authority, Canton Ticino
- Amount: +1.35 meters (ordinary), +1.40 meters (summer)

Ticino State Councillor Norman Gobbi commented on the rise of the maximum level of Lake Maggiore to +1.35 meters above the zero hydrometric level of Sesto Calende, defining it as a "comprehensive balanced solution." The decision was made as part of the experimental program on the regulation of Lake Maggiore levels, conducted by the Po River Basin Authority. The agreed ordinary maximum level remains set at +1.35 meters, while the summer limit of +1.40 meters can only be considered in case of water severity.

### Reactions and Criticisms
The Swiss authorities were not consulted and, consequently, were not aware of the decision to extend the summer maximum level to +1.40 meters. Canton Ticino immediately reported this breach of agreements to the Federal Office for the Environment (UFAM), asking it to intervene with the competent Italian authorities. Gobbi stated that the level of +1.35 meters is the result of a compromise matured through dialogue between Italian and Swiss institutions, within the framework of the bilateral body.

### Implications for Canton Ticino
A possible rise in the lake level can lead to various critical issues in Ticino territory. From an environmental point of view, it can affect the balance of riverside ecosystems. Moreover, in case of flooding, the hydraulic safety margin is reduced, increasing the risk for riverside areas. Lastly, higher levels can create difficulties in the use of the shores, with repercussions on the accessibility of public spaces and recreational and tourist activities.

### Safety and Water Management
The adopted solution takes into account the need not to compromise the safety of the riverside territories. The rise of the level to +1.35 meters is indeed limited until the end of July, ensuring that in the months of August and September the lake has an adequate regulation margin, with a maximum level set at +1.00 meter. This allows maintaining an absorption capacity useful to contain possible floods.

### Next Steps
State Councillor Gobbi reiterated the importance of constant dialogue between Italian and Swiss institutions to ensure a balanced and safe management of Lake Maggiore's water resources. UFAM has been tasked with intervening with the Italian authorities to resolve the breach of agreements and ensure compliance with the established procedures. - The rise in the water level of Lake Maggiore by +1.35 meters has significant implications for cross-border workers and residents along the lake's shores. In particular, changes in maximum levels can affect hydraulic safety and the use of public spaces.

### Impact on Hydraulic Safety
The rise in the lake's water level can reduce the hydraulic safety margin, increasing the risk of flooding. This is particularly relevant for lakeside areas, which could be more exposed to flooding during high water events. Ticino authorities have emphasized the importance of maintaining an adequate regulation margin to contain potential floods, especially during the summer months.

### Use of Public Spaces
Higher water levels in the lake can create difficulties in accessing the shores, affecting the accessibility of public spaces and recreational and tourist activities. This is particularly important as we approach the swimming season. Ticino authorities are evaluating the necessary measures to ensure the safety and usability of public spaces along the lake's shores.

### Dialogue Between Institutions
Councilor of State Gobbi reiterated the importance of constant dialogue between Italian and Swiss institutions to ensure balanced and safe management of Lake Maggiore's water resources. Ticino authorities have reported violations of agreements to the Federal Office for the Environment (FOEN), asking them to intervene with the competent Italian authorities to resolve the situation.

### Next Steps
Ticino authorities are closely monitoring the situation and collaborating with Italian institutions to ensure safe and sustainable management of Lake Maggiore's water resources. It is crucial to maintain constant dialogue and respect the established agreements to avoid further violations and ensure the safety of lakeside areas.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For cross-border workers and residents along the shores of Lake Maggiore, it is important to be aware of the implications of the rise in the lake's water level. The Ticino authorities are working to ensure the safety and usability of public spaces, but it is crucial to follow the authorities' instructions and respect safety measures.

### What to do in case of flooding
In case of flooding, it is important to follow the instructions of local authorities and respect safety measures. The Ticino authorities are closely monitoring the situation and will provide real-time updates. It is essential to avoid approaching the lake shores and follow the instructions to ensure your safety.

### Enjoyment of public spaces
The Ticino authorities are evaluating the necessary measures to ensure the enjoyment of public spaces along the lake shores. It is important to respect the authorities' instructions and follow safety measures to avoid accidents. Recreational and tourist activities will be closely monitored to ensure everyone's safety.

### Dialogue and collaboration
Dialogue between Italian and Swiss institutions is fundamental to ensure a balanced and safe management of Lake Maggiore's water resources. The Ticino authorities are collaborating with Italian institutions to resolve the violation of agreements and ensure compliance with established procedures. It is important to maintain constant dialogue and respect agreements to avoid further violations and ensure the safety of the lakeside territories.

### Useful tools
For further information and to monitor the situation of Lake Maggiore, you can consult the [lake level calculator](nav:calculator) and follow the updates from local authorities. It is essential to stay informed and follow the authorities' instructions to ensure your safety and that of others.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/relazioni-italo-svizzere/gobbi-sul-lago-maggiore-una-soluzione-complessivamente-equilibrata/91347280)*

item.title: Jans criticizes UDC initiative: 'Risk for Switzerland'
item.description: Federal Councillor Jans criticizes the UDC initiative 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million', warning of serious consequences for the country.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Jans criticizes UDC initiative 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million'
- Risk to bilateral agreements with the EU
- Possible serious economic and social consequences

## Key facts
- **What**: Criticism of the UDC initiative
- When: May 1, 2026
- Where: Bienne
- Who: Federal Councillor Beat Jans
- Amount: Not specified

Federal Councillor Beat Jans has issued a stern warning against the UDC's popular initiative 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million!'. According to Jans, the acceptance of this initiative could have serious consequences for Switzerland. The head of the Federal Department of Justice and Police (DFJP) spoke in Bienne on the occasion of a speech delivered for May Day.

The text of the initiative promises solutions to problems deeply felt by the population, such as overcrowded trains, traffic jams, rents, crime, and environmental difficulties. However, Jans argues that these are 'empty promises'. According to him, if approved, the initiative would oblige Switzerland to denounce the agreement on the free movement of persons, thus endangering the bilateral path with the European Union.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The UDC initiative could have a significant impact on cross-border workers employed in Switzerland. The denunciation of the agreement on the free movement of persons could complicate the procedures for obtaining the G permit and influence the working and living conditions of cross-border workers. Furthermore, the possible labor shortage could affect job opportunities for cross-border workers.

### Comparisons with the current situation

Currently, cross-border workers benefit from bilateral agreements that facilitate their access to the Swiss labor market. The denunciation of these agreements could lead to greater bureaucracy and more restrictive conditions for cross-border workers. Furthermore, the possible labor shortage could increase competition for jobs, making it more difficult for cross-border workers to find employment in Switzerland.

### Future scenarios

If the UDC initiative were approved, Switzerland could face a series of challenges. In addition to economic and social consequences, Switzerland could lose its international credibility and its humanitarian tradition. Furthermore, the possible labor shortage could have a negative impact on the Swiss economy, affecting key sectors such as tourism and industry.

### Analysis of Jans' statements

Jans emphasized that the UDC's proposal represents 'a great risk' for Switzerland. According to him, accepting the initiative would mean betraying the fundamental values of the country and compromising the reputation of the Confederation as a reliable state committed to the defense of human rights. Jans also warned that the proposal would damage Switzerland's international credibility and its humanitarian tradition.

### Conclusion

Jans' statements highlight the potential negative consequences of the UDC initiative for Switzerland. It is important that Swiss citizens are aware of the implications of this initiative and its possible consequences for the country and for cross-border workers employed in Switzerland. - ## Practical implications for cross-border workers

The UDC initiative 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million!' could have a significant impact on cross-border workers employed in Switzerland. The denunciation of the agreement on the free movement of persons could complicate the procedures for obtaining the G permit and influence the working and living conditions of cross-border workers. Additionally, the possible labor shortage could affect job opportunities for cross-border workers.

### Working and living conditions

The denunciation of the agreement on the free movement of persons could lead to increased bureaucracy and more restrictive conditions for cross-border workers. Additionally, the possible labor shortage could increase competition for jobs, making it more difficult for cross-border workers to find employment in Switzerland.

### Comparisons with the current situation

Currently, cross-border workers benefit from bilateral agreements that facilitate their access to the Swiss labor market. The denunciation of these agreements could lead to increased bureaucracy and more restrictive conditions for cross-border workers. Additionally, the possible labor shortage could increase competition for jobs, making it more difficult for cross-border workers to find employment in Switzerland.

### Future scenarios

If the UDC initiative is approved, Switzerland could face a series of challenges. In addition to the economic and social consequences, Switzerland could lose its international credibility and its humanitarian tradition. Furthermore, the possible labor shortage could have a negative impact on the Swiss economy, affecting key sectors such as tourism and industry.

### Analysis of Jans' statements

Jans emphasized that the UDC proposal represents 'a great risk' for Switzerland. According to him, accepting the initiative would mean betraying the fundamental values of the country and compromising Switzerland's reputation as a reliable state committed to the defense of human rights. Jans also warned that the proposal would damage Switzerland's international credibility and its humanitarian tradition.

### Conclusion

Jans' statements highlight the potential negative consequences of the UDC initiative for Switzerland. It is important that Swiss citizens are aware of the implications of this initiative and its possible consequences for the country and for cross-border workers employed in Switzerland.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do if the UDC initiative is approved

If the UDC initiative 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million!' is approved, cross-border workers employed in Switzerland should be prepared for possible changes in the procedures to obtain the G permit and in working and living conditions. It is important that cross-border workers are informed about the possible consequences of this initiative and the measures they can take to protect their rights and job opportunities.

### Steps to follow

1. **Inform yourself**: Cross-border workers should inform themselves about the possible consequences of the UDC initiative and the measures they can take to protect their rights and job opportunities.
2. Consult an expert: Cross-border workers should consult an expert in labor law and immigration to get advice on how to deal with possible changes in the procedures for obtaining the G permit and in working and living conditions.
3. Prepare: Cross-border workers should prepare for possible changes in the procedures for obtaining the G permit and in working and living conditions. This might include gathering additional documents and looking for new job opportunities.
4. Stay updated: Cross-border workers should stay updated on the latest news and developments regarding the UDC initiative and its possible consequences for cross-border workers employed in Switzerland.

### Useful tools

- Health insurance comparator: To compare different health insurance options and choose the one that best suits your needs.

item.title: Sesto Calende: fines for roads damaged by fiber optic sites
item.description: The local police of Sesto Calende have started checks and fines for roads damaged by fiber optic construction sites, funded by the PNRR.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Checks and fines for roads damaged by construction sites in Sesto Calende
- PNRR-funded interventions for fiber optics
- Local police issued fines to the executing company

## Key facts
- **What**: Checks and fines for roads damaged by construction sites
- When: April 22, 2026
- Where: Sesto Calende, Italy
- Who: Local police and Technical Office of Sesto Calende
- Amount: Not specified

Citizens' reports and delays in restoring road surfacing prompted the administration of Sesto Calende to carry out rigorous controls through the local police and technical office. On Wednesday, April 22, work finally began to repair the road surface in areas where fiber optic laying had left open excavations or inadequate temporary coverings.

As explained by Mayor Elisabetta Giordani's council, 'the interventions are not under the direct control of the municipal offices, but fall under national-level projects funded through the PNRR, aimed at covering the so-called “grey areas” with high-speed network infrastructure. Moreover, companies operating in the telecommunications sector act within the scope of the relevant national legislation, the so-called Telecommunications Decree, in force since 2003, which recognizes public utility and primary importance to these interventions, consequently limiting the margins of direct intervention by the Municipalities, even in terms of requesting guarantees or bonds.'

The Administration defined the management of the past weeks as 'unacceptable,' emphasizing how the lack of timely restorations created potential damage and hazards for circulation and users. The Public Works Office repeatedly urged the executing company without obtaining satisfactory responses.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers using the roads of Sesto Calende may experience temporary disruptions due to restoration work. It is important to pay attention to the construction sites and follow the instructions of the local police to avoid accidents. Additionally, cross-border workers residing in neighboring areas may be affected by similar work, as the PNRR provides for large-scale interventions for fiber optic laying.

### Control procedure and fines

The local police carried out thorough verification activities across the entire territory, ascertaining situations of non-restoration and issuing several fines to the executing company, with the application of penalties. In parallel, the Administration is working to define a plan of interventions on the most deteriorated roads in the city, to be implemented by the end of the mandate. ## Analysis of Critical Issues

The situation in Sesto Calende highlights the critical issues related to the management of roadworks and the maintenance of infrastructure. Although the PNRR is a nationally significant project, it presents significant challenges in terms of coordination between local authorities and executing companies. The lack of direct control by the municipalities limits the ability to intervene promptly in case of delays or negligence.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the intervention of the local police, the roads in Sesto Calende were in poor condition due to open roadworks and inadequate temporary road surfaces. The situation caused inconvenience for citizens and cross-border workers, with potential risks to road safety. The intervention of the local police represents an important step in resolving these critical issues and ensuring better maintenance of the infrastructure.

### Future Scenarios

With the start of the restoration work, an improvement in road conditions is expected by the end of the administrative term. However, it is fundamental that the executing companies respect the deadlines and regulations to avoid further inconvenience. The Administration of Sesto Calende is working to define a plan for interventions on the most deteriorated roads, which could also include collaboration with other companies to ensure more effective maintenance.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

Cross-border workers using the roads of Sesto Calende may experience temporary inconvenience due to the restoration work. It is important to pay attention to the roadworks and follow the instructions of the local police to avoid accidents. Furthermore, cross-border workers residing in neighboring areas may be affected by similar work, as the PNRR provides for large-scale interventions for the laying of fiber optics.

### Control Procedure and Sanctions

The local police have carried out thorough checks throughout the territory, verifying situations of non-restoration and issuing several reports against the executing company, with the application of sanctions. In parallel, the Administration is working to define a plan for interventions on the most deteriorated roads in the city, to be implemented by the end of the term.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for citizens and cross-border workers

For the citizens and cross-border workers of Sesto Calende, it is important to follow the instructions of the local police and the municipal administration to avoid inconveniences during the restoration works. It is advisable to inform yourself about the ongoing construction sites and plan alternative routes to avoid delays and accidents.

### Procedure to report inconveniences

In case of inconveniences or problems related to the construction sites, citizens and cross-border workers can report the situation to the local police or the technical office of the municipality. It is important to provide precise details about the location of the construction site and the nature of the problem to allow a timely intervention.

### Planning of works

The Administration of Sesto Calende is working to define a plan of interventions on the most deteriorated roads of the city. This plan could include collaboration with other companies to ensure more effective and timely maintenance. It is fundamental that citizens and cross-border workers are informed about the ongoing works and the completion times to plan their movements in the best way.

### Use of the site's tools

For further information and to plan their movements, cross-border workers can use the [route calculator](nav:calculator) available on the site. This tool allows you to view the ongoing construction sites and plan alternative routes to avoid inconveniences.

### Conclusion

The intervention of the local police of Sesto Calende represents an important step to solve the critical issues related to road construction sites and guarantee a better maintenance of the infrastructures. It is fundamental that citizens and cross-border workers collaborate with the municipal administration to guarantee the success of the interventions and improve the quality of life in the city.
